THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 5:42 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF DODSON, HAD A PATH LENGTH AROUND 1.8 MILES, A PATH WIDTH OF 125 YARDS, AND WAS RATED EFO WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 85 MPH. THE TORNADO TRAVELED ACROSS MOSTLY OPEN COUNTRY, DAMAGED TREES ALONG ITS PATH AND PRODUCED DAMAGE TO A LARGE SHEET METAL AND WOOD POLE BARN. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 5:48 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF DODSON.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT A FEW PIECES WERE TORN OFF OF A TIN ROOF AND A COUPLE OF TREES WERE DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 5:54 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE WEST SOUTHWEST OF DODSON, HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1.9 MILES, A PATH WIDTH OF 180 YARDS, AND WAS RATED EF0 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 85 MPH. LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED, AND A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED. THERE WAS DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND PORCHES OF SEVERAL HOMES AND SOME WEAK OUT BUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. ONE UNANCHORED TRAVEL TRAILER ROLLED 40 FEET AND WAS DESTROYED, AND A FEW POWER POLES WERE BROKEN. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 6:01 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE SOUTHEAST OF DODSON.

SEVERAL REPORTS OF DAMAGE CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS UP TO 90 MPH INCLUDED DAMAGE TO SEVERAL POLE BARNS AND OUT BUILDINGS. SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN AND A FEW DISEASED TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK OR UPROOTED. A FEW CENTER PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE ALSO FLIPPED. A CAMPER TRAILER WAS ROLLED AND SEVERAL OTHERS SLID SIDEWAYS. DAMAGE EXHIBITED A DIVERGENT PATTERN, SUGGESTING A STRONG DOWNBURST OCCURRED. DAMAGE PATH ENDED AROUND 4:32 AM ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHEAST OF GOODWELL.

SEVEN POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH POWER LINES ON THE GROUND AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 70 AND FARM TO MARKET ROAD 281. THIS WAS A PUBLIC REPORT RELAYED BY THE OCHILTREE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR DATA.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS REPORT FROM BORGER. A HOTEL ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF AND BLOWN ONTO A PARKING LOT AND CAUSED ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO A COUPLE PARKED VEHICLES 200 YARDS AWAY. AT A NEARBY CAR DEALERSHIP, A CAMPER TRAILER WAS ROLLED THROUGH A FENCE ACROSS AN ALLEY, AND AN OUTSIDE BILLBOARD SNAPPED OFF ITS 4 LARGE WOOD POLE SUPPORT AND FELL ONTO A BOAT LOCATED ON THE LOT OF THE DEALERSHIP. ALL DAMAGE OCCURRED IN A ONE HALF MILE WIDE AREA. PEAK WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 85 MPH. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WINDOWS BLOWN OUT, HOTEL ROOF BLOWN ONTO PARKING LOT, MAJOR DAMAGE TO VEHICLES DUE TO THE DESTROYED ROOF. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

THE ROOF OF A WELL HOUSE WAS BLOWN OFF AND HIT A GAZEBO, CAUSING DAMAGE. THE WELL HOUSE ITSELF WAS BLOWN OVER. BARN DOORS WERE DAMAGED, AND A FEW LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.

A LANDSPOUT CAUSED DAMAGE TO AN EXTENDED CARPORT. IN ADDITION, A COUPLE TRACTOR TRAILERS WERE PUSHED 30 TO 40 FEET FROM THEIR ORIGINAL LOCATION AND A ROOF WAS PARTIALLY BLOWN OFF A MOBILE HOME WHICH WAS ADJACENT TO THE DAMAGED EXTENDED CARPORT. PICTURES AND VIDEOS RECEIVED CONFIRM A LANDSPOUT.
